fullName:
com.blockether.spel.daemon-cdp-discovery-test.Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL..WSL with Windows-projected DTAP source.returns [loopback] when gateway lookup fails
name:
Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL. > WSL with Windows-projected DTAP source > returns [loopback] when gateway lookup fails

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL.

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.daemon-cdp-discovery-test.Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL..WSL with Windows-projected DTAP source.does not duplicate loopback if the gateway IP is also 127.0.0.1
name:
Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL. > WSL with Windows-projected DTAP source > does not duplicate loopback if the gateway IP is also 127.0.0.1

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL.

labels.name: testMethod
labels.value: does not duplicate loopback if the gateway IP is also 127.0.0.1

steps.name: allows both keys simultaneously (Playwright keeps whichever was set last internally)

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..both keys together.allows both keys simultaneously (Playwright keeps whichever was set last internally)
name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > both keys together > allows both keys simultaneously (Playwright keeps whichever was set last internally)

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all.

labels.name: testMethod
labels.value: allows both keys simultaneously (Playwright keeps whichever was set last internally)

steps.name: works for Paths with JSON-like substrings

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..:storage-state-path → setStorageStatePath.works for Paths with JSON-like substrings
name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state-path → setStorageStatePath > works for Paths with JSON-like substrings

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all.

labels.name: testMethod
labels.value: works for Paths with JSON-like substrings

steps.name: writes only the inline JSON field (not the path field)

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..:storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON).writes only the inline JSON field (not the path field)
name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON) > writes only the inline JSON field (not the path field)

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:

steps.name: handles JSON with escaped slashes and URLs

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..:storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON).handles JSON with escaped slashes and URLs
name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON) > handles JSON with escaped slashes and URLs

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all.

labels.name: testMethod
labels.value: handles JSON with escaped slashes and URLs

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.cli-test.Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly..scalar bridge results.renders a number scalar
name:
Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly. > scalar bridge results > renders a number scalar

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly.

" ";; Auto-generated by spel codegen
" ";; Source: Playwright JSONL recording
" ";; =============================================================================
" "
" "(ns my-app.generated-test
" "  "Auto-generated Playwright test."
" "  (:require
" "   [com.blockether.spel.assertions :as assert]
" "   [com.blockether.spel.core :as core]
" "   [com.blockether.spel.page :as page]
" "   [com.blockether.spel.roles :as role]
" "   [com.blockether.spel.allure :refer [defdescribe it expect]]))
" "
" "(defdescribe generated-test
" "  (it "recorded test"
" "    (core/with-testing-page {:headless false} [pg]
" "      ;; New page: pg
" "      (page/navigate pg "https://example.org/")
" "      (assert/contains-text (assert/assert-that (page/get-by-role pg role/heading)) "Example Domain")
" "      (core/close-page! pg))))
"))
produces exact output for example.org recording

steps.name: leaves both fields nil when neither key is present

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..neither key.leaves both fields nil when neither key is present
name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > neither key > leaves both fields nil when neither key is present

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all.

labels.name: testMethod
labels.value: leaves both fields nil when neither key is present

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.cli-test.Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly..map results still dispatch.renders :url map
name:
Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly. > map results still dispatch > renders :url map

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly.

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.cli-integration-test.Ultimate compatibility test: codegen generates Clojure, SCI evaluates it.
   Verifies that ALL codegen output formats produce valid code and that
   the :script format runs correctly in SCI eval-sci mode..codegen :script format evaluates in SCI.NEGATIVE: wrong assertText throws in SCI eval (not silently swallowed)
name:
Ultimate compatibility test: codegen generates Clojure, SCI evaluates it.
   Verifies that ALL codegen output formats produce valid code and that
   the :script format runs correctly in SCI eval-sci mode. > codegen :script format evaluates in SCI > NEGATIVE: wrong assertText throws in SCI eval (not silently swallowed)

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Ultimate compatibility test: codegen generates Clojure, SCI evaluates it.

name:
Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`

   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly. > map results still dispatch > renders :title map

Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly.

steps.name: handles JSON containing cookie paths (the bug that motivated this split)

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..:storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON).handles JSON containing cookie paths (the bug that motivated this split)
name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON) > handles JSON containing cookie paths (the bug that motivated this split)

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all.

labels.name: testMethod
labels.value: handles JSON containing cookie paths (the bug that motivated this split)

steps.name: writes only the path field (not the inline JSON field)

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..:storage-state-path → setStorageStatePath.writes only the path field (not the inline JSON field)
name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state-path → setStorageStatePath > writes only the path field (not the inline JSON field)

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all.

labels.name: testMethod
labels.value: writes only the path field (not the inline JSON field)

   HTTP exchange markdown / cURL command is fully reproducible from the
   Allure report..Authenticated JSON API calls.issues GET and POST with auth, content-type, correlation headers and JSON body
name:
Showcase: API calls with custom request headers and JSON bodies so the
   HTTP exchange markdown / cURL command is fully reproducible from the
   Allure report. > Authenticated JSON API calls > issues GET and POST with auth, content-type, correlation headers and JSON body

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Showcase: API calls with custom request headers and JSON bodies so the
   HTTP exchange markdown / cURL command is fully reproducible from the
   Allure report.

labels.name: testMethod
labels.value: issues GET and POST with auth, content-type, correlation headers and JSON body

description:
Exercises the full request-meta capture: Authorization bearer
          token, Content-Type, Accept, User-Agent and an X-Request-ID
          correlation header, plus a JSON request body on POST. The
          resulting HTTP markdown attachment must include a Request
          Headers section, a Request Body section, and a cURL command
          with `-H` flags + `-d` payload so a developer can replay the
          call straight from the report.

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.cli-test.Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly..scalar bridge results.renders a bare url string
name:
Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly. > scalar bridge results > renders a bare url string

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Rendering of CLI results, including scalar bridge responses.

   A bridge-routed get text/value/title/url can return a bare string (not a
   daemon-shaped map). Every dispatch branch in print-result assumes `data`
   is a map, so a scalar used to crash with `contains? not supported on type:
   java.lang.String`. Scalars must now render cleanly.

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.daemon-cdp-discovery-test.Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL..WSL with Windows-projected DTAP source.returns [loopback, gateway] when gateway resolves
name:
Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL. > WSL with Windows-projected DTAP source > returns [loopback, gateway] when gateway resolves

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL.

description:
Fetches JSON data from the API test server, then injects it into
        a browser page as HTML and verifies the DOM rendering.
        Uses (.request (.context pg)) (from BrowserContext.request()) so API calls
        appear in the Playwright trace alongside browser interactions.
        Demonstrates combining api-step (HTTP calls) with ui-step
        (browser interactions with before/after screenshots).

steps.name: returns ["127.0.0.1"] — no gateway probe needed for a truly local browser

fullName:
com.blockether.spel.daemon-cdp-discovery-test.Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL..WSL with native-Linux DTAP source (wsl? true, path under ~/.config).returns ["127.0.0.1"] — no gateway probe needed for a truly local browser
name:
Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL. > WSL with native-Linux DTAP source (wsl? true, path under ~/.config) > returns ["127.0.0.1"] — no gateway probe needed for a truly local browser

labels.name: subSuite
labels.value:
Unit tests for cdp-candidate-hosts — the per-source host-list builder
   that decides whether spel probes loopback-only or loopback + WSL
   default gateway.

   `wsl?` is a private var backed by `System/getenv` + a file read, both
   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL.

labels.name: testMethod
labels.value: returns ["127.0.0.1"] — no gateway probe needed for a truly local browser
